recalls.Description:
Scepter B62 gas and oil fuel containers are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These containers are red with black caps and yellow spouts, featuring dual chambers with a 6-liter capacity for gas and 2.5 liters for oil. The words 'Scepter' and 'Essence Danger' are embossed on the side, indicating their model. The containers do not meet mandatory safety standards, lacking essential flame mitigation devices and child-resistant features, which increases the risk of flash fires and potential poisoning for children.

Consumers are urged to stop using these recalled containers immediately and keep them out of children's reach. Mameix Retail is offering a full refund, and customers must label the container as 'Recalled' and provide a photo of it in the trash to complete the return process.
recalls.Title: Scepter Fuel Containers Recalled Over Fire and Poisoning Risks; Violates Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Mameix Retail at 587-816-2804 from 11 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email scepterrecall@gmail.com.

Products.Name: Scepter Gas and Oil Combination Fuel Container

Images.Caption: Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – front
Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – front

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com between August 2024 and May 2025 for approximately $40.

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire and child poisoning due to lack of safety features.

Remedies.Name: Stop using immediately and contact for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Scepter fuel containers due to fire and poisoning hazards. Contact Mameix Retail for refunds and safety guidance.

recalls.Description: Bazic Products is recalling its Bazic Silicone Glue, Model 2030, due to serious safety concerns. This glue is packaged in a clear plastic bottle featuring a blue nozzle and a wraparound label that includes safety warnings. The affected product has the batch code "V01233-031725-70004523" printed on the side. The glue's packaging fails to meet child-resistant standards, posing a risk of poisoning to children if ingested. Additionally, the product is incorrectly labeled as "Non-Toxic" and lacks a warning for being "highly flammable," violating federal regulations.
recalls.Title: Bazic Silicone Glue Recall Due to Poisoning Risk and Labeling Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Bazic Products at 877-772-2942 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday or via email at compliance@bazic.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bazic Silicone Glue – Front and Side
Recalled Bazic Silicone Glue – Location of Batch Code

Retailers.Name: Available at Shims Bargain Inc., Henry Lamond Co. Ltd., Universal Wholesale, D'notions & crafts stores, and online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com between May 2025 and October 2025 for approximately $3.

Importers.Name: Bangkit USA Inc, dba Bazic Products, of El Monte, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging and incorrect labeling

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product immediately and contact Bazic Products for a refund. Dispose of the glue according to local hazardous waste regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Bazic Products recalls Bazic Silicone Glue due to poisoning risks and labeling violations. Learn how to get a refund and dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description: The recall affects Iristar Minoxidil Spray Bottles, designed for hair and beard growth, which contain 5% minoxidil and biotin. These products are packaged in black plastic bottles featuring navy blue labels and white accents, with the name "Iristar" prominently displayed. The back of the packaging includes essential information such as ingredients and usage instructions. The current packaging fails to meet child-resistant standards, posing a significant risk of poisoning to young children if ingested.
recalls.Title: Iristar Minoxidil Spray Bottles Recalled for Child Safety Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Moralowen at 862-397-7376 during business hours, email moralowens@gmail.com, or visit moralowen.com and select "Recall Details" at the top of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Iristar Minoxidil Spray Bottles with packaging
Recalled Iristar Minoxidil Spray Bottles

Manufacturers.Name: Shaoshan Leiluo Trading Co., Ltd. of Shaoshan, Hunan of China

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase on Amazon.com from December 2024 to September 2025 for approximately $18.

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging leading to potential poisoning risk for children

Remedies.Name: Consumers should secure the recalled bottles away from children and request a free replacement with a child-resistant closure from Moralowen. After transferring the contents, the product can be used as directed.

recalls.metaDescription: Iristar Minoxidil Spray Bottles recalled due to child safety risks. Contact Moralowen for a replacement with child-resistant packaging.

recalls.Description: Plantimex is recalling Mamisan Pain Relieving Topical Ointment due to the risk of serious harm or fatality from child poisoning. The ointment is packaged in an orange plastic jar with a white lid, featuring the Mamisan trademark on the lid and label. This recall specifically affects jars with the UPC code 860006498115, which contains instructions and drug facts on its wraparound label.
recalls.Title: Plantimex Recalls Mamisan Lidocaine Ointment Due to Child Safety Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Plantimex at 855-752-6869 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email customercare@plantimexusa.com with the subject "RECALL". More information is available at http://plantimexusa.com/contact.php or www.plantimexusa.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Mamisan Licodaine Ointment jar UPC - 860006498115
Recalled Mamisan Lidocaine Ointment 3.52 ounce jar
Recalled Mamisan Lidocaine Ointment jar non-child resistant packaging (continuous thread lid)

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart and Target stores nationwide and online at Target.com from April 2024 through October 2025 for approximately $10.

Distributors.Name: Plantimex Distributors, Inc., of San Diego, California

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant packaging of lidocaine ointment poses poisoning risks to young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should store the recalled ointment jars out of children's reach and contact Plantimex for a free replacement lid. The product can be used safely with the new lid.

recalls.metaDescription: Plantimex recalls Mamisan Lidocaine Ointment due to child safety risks. Contact for a free replacement lid to ensure safe use.

recalls.Description: iHerb has announced a recall for three varieties of California Gold Nutrition dietary supplements: Daily Prenatal Multi, Ultamins Women's Multivitamin, and Ultamins Women's 50+ Multivitamin. The Daily Prenatal Multi is packaged in a white bottle with a white lid and gold border, containing 60 fish gelatin softgel capsules. The Ultamins Women's Multivitamin and Women's 50+ Multivitamin come in dark purple packaging with a gold border, each containing 60 capsules in foil. Specific batch numbers and expiration dates are printed on the back of each package. The affected products include batch codes with expiration dates ranging from May 2026 to November 2026.
recalls.Title: iHerb Issues Recall for California Gold Nutrition Iron Supplements Due to Child Poisoning Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach iHerb toll-free at 888-430-4770, email ProductRecall@iherb.com, or visit https://www.iherb.com/help/contact or https://information.iherb.com/hc/en-us/articles/37894456653972-iHerb-Recalls-California-Gold-Nutrition-Iron-Supplements-Due-to-Risk-of-Child-Poisoning.

Products.Name: California Gold Nutrition Daily Prenatal Multi

Images.Caption: Recalled Daily Prenatal Multi bottle
Label for Recalled Daily Prenatal Multi
Recalled Ultamins Women's Multivitamin package
Label for recalled Ultamins Women's Multivitamin package
Recalled Ultamins Women's 50+ Multivitamin package
Label for recalled Ultamins Women's 50+ Multivitamin package

Manufacturers.Name: iHerb LLC, dba Madre Labs LLC, of Irvine, California

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Target.com, and other retailers from January 2019 to April 2025, priced between $8 and $18.

Hazards.Name: Risk of child poisoning due to non-child-resistant packaging for iron-containing supplements.

Remedies.Name: Secure the recalled products out of children's reach and contact iHerb for a refund and disposal instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: iHerb recalls California Gold Nutrition Iron Supplements due to child poisoning risk. Secure products and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description:
Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (NFH) has issued a recall for its Iron SAP, Heme Iron SAP, and Prenatal SAP dietary supplement bottles. These bottles are white and feature the NFH brand name along with a blue leaf logo. The date codes can be found on the bottom of the bottles. The recalled products include various sizes and descriptions, with specific date codes indicating their recall status.

The Iron SAP is available in both 60 and 120 capsule sizes, while Heme Iron SAP comes in a 60 capsule size. Prenatal SAP is offered in a 180 capsule size. Consumers are urged to check their bottles against the provided date codes to determine if they are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of NFH Iron Dietary Supplements Due to Non-Child Resistant Packaging
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach NFH at 866-510-3123 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at info@nfh.ca. More details can be found on their website at https://nfh.ca/ by clicking on the 'Recall' section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Iron SAP Iron Glycinate (120 capsules 1167U)
Recalled Iron SAP Iron Glycinate (60 Capsules 1029U)
Recalled Heme Iron SAP Superior Absorption and bioavailability (60 capsules 1124U)
Recalled Prenatal SAP Multivitamin (180 capsules 1034U)
Please see bottom of bottle for lot and expiration: (Example)

Manufacturers.Name: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (dba NFH), of Canada

Retailers.Name: Available at various naturopathic and homeopathic clinics nationwide, A Woman's Time, Doctor Supplement stores, and online at WholescriptsInc.com and Fullscript.com from March 2022 through December 2024, priced between $20 and $95 based on product and size.

Importers.Name: Nutritional Fundamentals for Health (dba NFH), of Canada

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to non-child resistant packaging, violating the Poison Prevention Packaging Act (PPPA).

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the recalled bottles out of children's reach and contact NFH for a free replacement child-resistant bottle. NFH is reaching out to all known buyers regarding this recall.

recalls.metaDescription: NFH recalls Iron SAP, Heme Iron SAP, and Prenatal SAP dietary supplements due to non-compliant packaging posing poisoning risks.

recalls.Description: Taylor Water Technologies is recalling its Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles that were sold with the Taylor Phosphate Test Kits #K-1106, as well as those sold separately. These kits are designed for testing phosphate levels in water for pools, spas, and aquariums. The affected bottles contain sulfuric acid and are labeled as "Phosphate Reagent #1" with part number "R-0980," and they have a shelf-life of 24 months. Due to non-compliance with the Poison Prevention Packaging Act, the packaging does not meet child-resistant standards, posing a serious risk of chemical burns.
recalls.Title: Taylor Water Technologies Recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 Bottles Due to Chemical Burn Risks and Non-Compliance with Child-Resistant Packaging Regulations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Taylor Water Technologies at 800-837-8548 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also fill out the online contact form at https://www.taylortechnologies.com/en/content/contact-customer-service or visit https://www.taylortechnologies.com and navigate to "Recall Information" under the Resource Center tab for further details.

Products.Name: Phosphate Reagent #1 Bottles from Taylor Phosphate Test Kits

Images.Caption: Recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle sold within the Taylor Phosphate Test Kits
Taylor Phosphate Test Kit with recalled Phosphate Reagent #1 bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Taylor Water Technologies LLC, of Sparks, Maryland

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at www.taylortechnologies.com and other websites, as well as in pool and aquarium retail stores across the country from August 2022 through August 2024.

Hazards.Name: Risk of chemical burns due to non-child-resistant packaging of sulfuric acid-containing reagent bottles.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bottles, secure them away from children, and contact Taylor Water Technologies for disposal guidance and a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Taylor Water Technologies recalls Phosphate Reagent #1 bottles due to chemical burn risks. Learn how to get a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Cabinet Health is recalling specific 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottles due to concerns that the lids may not maintain child-resistant features. These bottles, made of clear glass with colored plastic lids, were sold as either filled with over-the-counter medications or as empty containers. The recall affects bottles purchased from March 2023 through July 2024, and consumers can identify the recalled products by their purchase date and specific date codes located on the bottom sticker of the bottles.
recalls.Title: Cabinet Health Recalls 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottles Due to Child Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Cabinet Health at 908-242-6108 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@wearecabinet.com, or visit https://cabinethealth.com/voluntaryrecall for more details.

Products.Name: Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle
Recalled Cabinet Health 4 oz. Refillable Medicine Bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Cabinet Health Inc., of Washington, D.C.

Retailers.Name: Available online at cabinethealth.com, Amazon.com, Grove.co, and in CVS stores from March 2023 through July 2024, priced between $10 and $20.

Hazards.Name: Lid closure may deteriorate over time, reducing child-resistance and increasing the risk of poisoning if accessed by children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the recalled bottles away from children and contact Cabinet Health for a free replacement lid. A photo showing the destroyed lid will be required. Cabinet Health and Amazon are reaching out to all known customers directly. This recall does not affect the medications inside the bottles.

recalls.metaDescription: Cabinet Health recalls 4 oz. refillable medicine bottles due to safety concerns. Contact for free replacement lid and more details.

recalls.Description: Procter & Gamble has issued a recall for specific lot codes of Tide Pods, Gain Flings, Ace Pods, and Ariel Pods liquid laundry detergent packets. These products, packaged in flexible film bags, were manufactured between September 2023 and February 2024 and pose a serious risk of injury, especially to children, if ingested. The recalled items range from bags containing 12 to 39 packets and include various scents and formulations.
recalls.Title: Procter & Gamble Recalls 8.2 Million Laundry Detergent Packets Due to Safety Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Procter & Gamble at 833-347-5764, available Monday through Friday from 9 AM to 6 PM ET, and Saturday from 9 AM to 5:30 PM ET. More information can also be found online at pg.com/bags.

Products.Name: Tide Pods
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8.2 million (In addition, about 56,741 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Tide Pods
Recalled Gain laundry packets
Recalled Ace Pods
Recalled Ariel laundry packets

Injuries.Name: No confirmed cases directly relating to this packaging defect. The firm has received four reports of children in the United States accessing the liquid laundry packets, three of which reported ingestion during the time period that the recalled lots were sold, but it is not known if these laundry packets came from recalled bags.

Manufacturers.Name: The Procter & Gamble Company, of Cincinnati, Ohio

Hazards.Name: The packaging may split open, risking ingestion by children and vulnerable individuals, which can lead to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should keep the recalled bags out of children's reach and contact Procter & Gamble for a full refund and a free child-resistant storage bag. A cabinet lock for securing laundry items is also available. To check if their product is affected, consumers can verify the lot code on the bottom of the package at pg.com/bags.

recalls.metaDescription: Procter & Gamble recalls 8.2 million laundry detergent packets due to safety risks. Check if your product is affected and learn how to get a refund.
